name: Generated Short-answer Labels Require Audit Before Cold-start Scorer Training
statement: Bexte and Zesch show that LLM-generated short answers can support cold-start content-scoring research only after label audit or manual relabeling; as-generated target labels underperform original student-response training data and can be highly nonconforming.
population_or_context: Short-answer supervised content scoring on the Student Response Analysis dataset.
quantitative_note: The paper reports macro-averaged F1 comparisons and states that up to 75% of generated answers requested for a specific label did not conform to that label.
caveats: Bounded to one SRA setup, five-way labels, DeepSeek-v2, and the tested zero-shot prompt.
Manual relabeling improves utility but adds human labor.
This is training-utility evidence, not final scorer-validation evidence.
evaluation_method: Label-conformance analysis, macro-averaged F1 on original student answers, and comparison between as-generated and manually cleaned labels.
reported_metrics: Macro-averaged F1 and label-conformance findings, including the reported up-to-75% nonconformance for requested labels.
limitations: Does not show generated target labels can be accepted without audit.
Does not generalize across all LLMs, subjects, or score scales.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases generated-label setup, macro-F1 comparisons, label nonconformance, and manual relabeling implications.
